_comment: DOME Registry example entry — v2.0.0. This is a fictionalised but realistic example of a fully completed DOME Registry entry for a supervised machine learning method in biology. In v2.0.0 all DOME content fields are structured objects with typed sub-fields (Boolean, Number, String, Array) rather than free-text strings. No real personal data or user account information is included. System fields (_id, user, uuid, shortid, __v, score, update, reviewState, created, updated) assigned server-side are shown here for illustrative completeness only; omit them when submitting a new entry.

publication.title: BindPredict: a random forest model for predicting protein–ligand binding residues from sequence features.
publication.authors: Example A, Demonstration B, Fictitious C.

splits.otherSplits: Inner 5-fold CV loop on training split for hyperparameter selection
splits.splitDist: Stratified random sampling maintaining 1:10 positive-to-negative class ratio

encoding.encMeth: Position-specific scoring matrix (PSSM) from PSI-BLAST (3 iterations against UniRef90); 20 aa × 11-residue window = 220 PSSM values concatenated with 6 mean physicochemical property values for the window

fit.noOverFit: Training AUROC 0.97 vs independent test AUROC 0.83; gap is consistent with Random Forest behaviour on imbalanced data and is discussed in Section 3.4
fit.noUnderFit: Training AUROC of 0.97 is sufficiently high to rule out underfitting

parameters.pSelMeth: 5-fold cross-validation grid search on training set; key settings: n_estimators=500, max_features='sqrt', max_depth=None, min_samples_split=2, class_weight='balanced'

duration.runTime: Approximately 12 minutes on a single Intel Xeon E5-2680 CPU core (no GPU required); inference on a 300-residue protein takes <1 second

interpretability.transpEx: Gini impurity-based feature importance scores reported for all 66 features (Supplementary Table S2); PSSM conservation scores and hydrophobicity identified as the most predictive features; no SHAP or other post-hoc method applied

output.outType: Binary classification per residue: binding (1) or non-binding (0), with an associated probability score in [0, 1]

method.evalMeth: 5-fold stratified cross-validation on the training set for model selection and hyperparameter tuning; final performance assessed on a fully held-out independent test set (20% of total data) not used during any stage of training or tuning
